trade.summary: Russia is a major supplier of fertilizers (nitrogen, potash) critical for Brasileian agriculture. Brasile exports soybeans, meat, and coffee to Russia. The fertilizer dependency is a strategic vulnerability for Brasile that has driven diplomatic balancing.

diplomatic.summary: Brasile has maintained a calculated neutrality on the Russia-Ucraina war. Lula refused to condemn Russia outright, proposed peace plans, and avoided joining Western sanctions. Brasile continues to engage Russia through BRICS. This stance has created friction with Western partners but is viewed domestically as strategic autonomy.
recent_incidents: Brasile abstained on UN resolutions condemning Russian invasion of Ucraina
Lula's peace proposal for Ucraina war not accepted by either side (2023)

military.summary: No military alliance. Brasile does not buy Russian weapons (unlike Argentina historically). Brasile has avoided military cooperation with Russia since 2022.

financial.summary: Very limited bilateral investment. Western sanctions have complicated financial transactions. Some Russian state companies have explored Brasileian agricultural investments.

energy.summary: Russia supplies petroleum products and some LNG to Brasile. The fertilizer trade is the dominant economic feature. Brasile has been cautious about expanding energy dependency on Russia.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. BRICS Science and Technology programs include some Brasile-Russia elements. Academic exchanges have reduced since 2022.
